payload.title: Foundations of Equivaria — M2 Review Run

payload.content:
{"summary":"The paper formulates order-equivariant neural networks for finite posets equipped with a finite group action and equivariant feature bundles. It characterizes linear equivariant maps through a transporter law and pair-orbit stabilizer intertwiners, then builds nonlinear models from orbital affine maps, Reynolds-equivariant pointwise blocks, and pair-orbit aggregation. The main theoretical results are a universal approximation theorem for an unrestricted full OENN class and a diameter-depth local universality result for source-labeled pair-state networks. Graph and cellular/simplicial sheaf constructions are presented as special or further-tied cases. A long appendix relates completed OENN maps to category-equivariant neural networks through finite action groupoids and reports one EMG-IMU experiment for a Grothendieck CENN.","strengthsAndWeaknesses":"Strength: The linear theory is explicit and useful: the transporter law, pair-orbit parametrization, and stabilizer intertwiners give a clean recipe for all equivariant block kernels, including nontrivial fiber transports. The Reynolds construction then supplies a principled nonlinear mechanism without assuming that raw coordinatewise activations commute with stabilizer actions.
Strength: The architecture hierarchy is clearly articulated. The paper separates anonymous relation message passing, source-labeled pair-state models, and unrestricted full models, and it gives a constructive diameter-depth broadcast compilation together with a matching worst-case locality lower bound.
Strength: The graph and sheaf examples carefully distinguish exact orbit-parametrized operators from more coarsely tied familiar layers, and they explicitly identify the expressive gap between conventional anonymous local message passing or sheaf diffusion and the pair-state completion.
Weakness (reviewer-r1-W1): The central framing is broader than the formal symmetry used by OENN. OENN equivariance is exactly equivariance to a finite group G acting on the total direct-sum feature space; the order supplies G-stable masks, incidence relations, and admissible automorphisms but is not itself an equivariance or naturality condition, as the paper later acknowledges. In the full class, the UAT broadcasts the whole input and applies site-stabilizer Reynolds MLPs. The manuscript therefore needs a theorem-level account of what is new beyond finite-group equivariant networks and an action-groupoid reformulation, particularly for posets with small or trivial automorphism groups.
Weakness (reviewer-r1-W2): The statement that the UAT is new even when restricted to sheaf neural networks is not matched by the architecture class actually proved universal. The full theorem permits global orbital mixing, while the local theorem reconstructs global access with source-labeled pair states that retain every source feature and propagate for diameter-many layers. The paper itself states that ordinary sheaf diffusion or sheaf message passing remains non-universal in general. The result is therefore a UAT for a broad sheaf-indexed OENN completion, not for conventional sheaf neural networks; the headline claim should be narrowed or a theorem for a recognizable sheaf-network class should be added.
Weakness (reviewer-r1-W3): The CENN portion is not self-contained enough for the breadth claimed. Definition A.2 delegates the arrow-bundle generators to Maruyama (2025a), and Theorem A.3 invokes standard CENN hypotheses without fully specifying or proving the machinery needed for the asserted general UAT. Much of the subsequent appendix establishes a finite action-groupoid specialization that is already covered by the direct OENN proof. The paper should clearly separate imported prior results from new results and either provide complete definitions and hypotheses or substantially narrow the CENN claims.
Weakness (reviewer-r1-W4): The empirical claims are not currently reliable. The paper supplies only a result table and a qualitative explanation, while the statistical validation bundle reports a non-like-for-like TemporalCNN comparison involving different compute, tuning, splits, and metric definitions, and an exact subject identifier shared between training and held-out data. It additionally finds fewer than two recorded seeds, no uncertainty estimate, and no multiplicity correction. These findings directly compromise the claimed outperformance and improved generalization, although they are ancillary to the OENN theory. A subject-disjoint, protocol-matched rerun with run-level uncertainty is needed.
Weakness (reviewer-r1-W5): The constructive local UAT has severe unquantified cost: every carrier stores source-labeled slots totaling the whole input dimension, depth scales with directed diameter, and the exact compilation uses pair-specific walk-count rescaling. The paper provides no parameter, compute, sample-complexity, optimization, or stability analysis and no empirical evaluation of this OENN construction. This limits the practical significance of the local universality result without invalidating its formal existence claim.","soundness":2,"presentation":3,"significance":2,"originality":2,"overallRecommendation":3,"confidence":4,"questions":["What is the precise theorem-level novelty over standard finite-group equivariant intertwiner constructions and universal approximation, and what additional role does the poset order play once unrestricted full orbital layers are allowed? Please compare assumptions and model classes directly with the closest cited work.","Which exact class of existing sheaf neural networks is covered by the claimed sheaf UAT? Is universality proved for conventional restriction/adjoint-based local sheaf layers, or only after adding global orbital layers or source-labeled pair-state memory?","Which CENN definitions and theorems are new in this submission rather than imported from Maruyama (2025a and related work), and can the full arrow-bundle generators and hypotheses of Theorem A.3 be stated self-containedly?","Can the EMG-IMU experiment be rerun with subject-disjoint partitions, identical metrics and data splits, matched compute and tuning budgets for all baselines, multiple independent seeds, uncertainty intervals, and correction or clear labeling for multiple comparisons? Please also explain the reported subject-2 overlap."],"limitations":"The main OENN results assume a finite poset, a finite acting group, finite-dimensional fibers, compact invariant approximation domains, and a continuous non-polynomial activation. Full universality uses global broadcast. Local universality requires a strongly connected communication relation, depth at least the directed diameter in the worst case, and source-labeled pair-state memory whose total width scales with the whole input at each carrier. The work gives no parameter or sample complexity, optimization guarantee, numerical stability analysis, or evidence across multiple domains. One-directional order-causal models and ordinary anonymous graph or sheaf message-passing subclasses are explicitly outside the arbitrary-map universality guarantee. The only reported experiment is a single CENN benchmark and has unresolved protocol and leakage concerns.","ethicalConcerns":"None reported."}

payload.reason: Independent initial review on frozen paper 34584.

payload.content:
{"summary":"The submission studies neural maps on finite posets equipped with finite-group actions and equivariant fiber transports. It derives a transporter-law and orbit-stabilizer parameterization of linear maps, constructs nonlinear layers using Reynolds-averaged pointwise blocks and pair-orbit aggregation, and proves a full universal approximation result by broadcasting the total input to each output site and applying stabilizer-equivariant MLPs. It also gives a source-labeled pair-state construction that realizes this broadcast over a strongly connected local relation in directed-diameter depth, specializes the constructions to fixed graphs and sheaf-indexed fibers, and relates the resulting finite action-groupoid formulation to a broader category-equivariant neural network framework. An appendix reports an EMG-IMU comparison for a Grothendieck CENN.","strengthsAndWeaknesses":"Strength: The linear theory is cleanly formulated at the bundle level: the transporter law handles nontrivial fiber transports, and the orbit parameterization correctly isolates pair-stabilizer intertwiner constraints. The accompanying proofs are concise, and the mathematics validator provides appropriately limited exact support for a representative specialization.
Strength: The main finite-group UAT is constructive and explicit about its central assumptions: finite-dimensional fibers, a compact invariant domain, and a continuous non-polynomial activation. The broadcast-plus-Reynolds proof is easy to audit, and the validator separately supports the theorem-scope statement and representative Reynolds calculations without overstating those checks as general proofs.
Strength: The pair-state local compilation is a concrete and useful construction. It exposes both the exact directed-diameter propagation depth and the source-labeled memory cost rather than hiding the global information requirement behind informal locality language.
Strength: The sheaf specialization carefully distinguishes lifted group actions, invariant fiber metrics, adjoint naturality, and stabilizer conditions. This is substantially more precise than simply asserting that a familiar sheaf update is equivariant.
Weakness (reviewer-r2-W1): The central notion called order-equivariance is, at the input-output level, ordinary equivariance for the finite-group representations on the total feature spaces: equation (2) contains no order or restriction map. Correspondingly, the full UAT uses only a global broadcast and stabilizer-equivariant approximation, and the stated sheaf-indexed consequence approximates all G-equivariant maps on total fibers rather than maps required to respect sheaf restrictions. Thus the theorem appears mathematically sound at its stated finite-group scope, but the advertised order-specific and sheaf-neural-network novelty is not established. This can be resolved by narrowing the claims and terminology, or by proving a universality result for a target class or architecture in which order or sheaf naturality is essential, together with a precise comparison to existing finite-group equivariant UATs.
Weakness (reviewer-r2-W2): Theorem 3.5 proves an information-propagation lower bound for architectures with at most L inter-site layers when a relevant source is farther than L. It does not prove that the defined relation-message-passing class is non-universal at unrestricted depth, nor that persistent source labels are necessary for universality. Moreover, the paper's orbit-wise relation-message-passing encoders can be richer than the additionally tied anonymous MPNN special case. Statements that anonymous aggregation is not universal in general and that a source-label-like mechanism is unavoidable therefore exceed the proved result. A precise unrestricted-depth architecture definition plus an indistinguishability counterexample would resolve this; otherwise the claims should be restricted to the established depth-versus-distance obstruction.
Weakness (reviewer-r2-W3): The finite action-groupoid naturality equivalence is understandable, but the broader CENN claims are not self-contained. Definition A.2 delegates the arrow-bundle generators to external work, Theorem A.3 invokes standard CENN hypotheses without a complete formal statement or proof here, and Lemma A.6 relies on properties of the externally defined UAT construction to assert that the needed approximants can be chosen as OENN primitives. Consequently, the claims that CENN gives the general form and that its UAT yields the asserted finite realization cannot be independently verified from this submission. The paper should supply complete generator definitions, exact analytic hypotheses, and a self-contained proof dependency, or clearly demote these statements to background from prior work.
Weakness (reviewer-r2-W4): The empirical evidence does not support the stated generalization conclusion. The code validator reports that one recorded key result reproduces, but the statistical validator finds that the TemporalCNN comparison uses materially different compute and tuning budgets, a different split, and a different metric definition; it also identifies subject-2 in both training and held-out data. Fewer than two recorded seeds, absent uncertainty estimates, and uncorrected multiple comparisons add further uncertainty. Reproducing a recorded number does not resolve these design defects. The claims require a subject-disjoint common split, identical metric definitions, matched resource and tuning budgets, repeated seeds, uncertainty reporting, and an explicit multiplicity policy.","soundness":2,"presentation":2,"significance":2,"originality":2,"overallRecommendation":3,"confidence":4,"questions":["What precise approximation target or architectural constraint makes the main result order- or sheaf-specific, rather than a finite-group equivariant UAT applied to a poset-indexed direct sum?","Can the authors provide an unrestricted-depth counterexample proving non-universality of the exact anonymous or relation-message-passing class they intend, rather than only the L-step propagation-radius lower bound?","Can the authors state all CENN arrow-bundle primitives and analytic hypotheses used in Theorem A.3 and prove, within the submission, the selection claim needed by Lemma A.6?","Can the EMG-IMU comparison be rerun with subject-disjoint identical splits, identical metrics, matched compute and tuning budgets, multiple seeds, uncertainty estimates, and a declared multiple-comparison policy?"],"limitations":"The theory is restricted to fixed finite posets and finite groups. Full universality uses a global broadcast, while local universality requires strong connectivity, diameter-scale depth, and source-labeled memory whose per-carrier width is the total input dimension. The submission does not establish scalability to large or varying-size structures, continuous or infinite symmetry groups, robustness, or practical gains for OENN itself. The only empirical appendix concerns a narrow wearable-sensor CENN setting and is not reliable under the reported validation findings.","ethicalConcerns":"None reported."}

payload.reason: Independent initial review on frozen paper 34584.

payload.content:
{"summary":"This submission formulates order-equivariant neural networks (OENNs) on finite posets equipped with finite-group actions and transported local feature fibers. It characterizes linear equivariant maps through pair-orbit transporter and stabilizer constraints, constructs nonlinear layers from orbital affine maps, Reynolds-equivariant blocks, and pair-orbit aggregation, and proves universal approximation for both a full architecture and a source-labeled local pair-state architecture under a diameter condition. It specializes the framework to fixed graphs and cellular or simplicial sheaf layers, relates completed OENN maps to CENN natural transformations over action groupoids, and includes an EMG-IMU case study.","strengthsAndWeaknesses":"Strength: The linear theory is explicit and constructive: the transporter law is paired with an orbit-representative parametrization and stabilizer intertwiner condition, and the appendix supplies direct proofs. The validator evidence supports the declared block shapes and a representative two-site transporter specialization, while appropriately limiting those checks to special cases.
Strength: The expressivity discussion usefully separates ordinary anonymous message passing, source-labeled pair-state locality, and unrestricted full OENNs. The local theorem combines a dependency lower bound with an exact diameter-depth broadcast construction and explicitly states the associated hidden-state growth, making the universality claim more informative than an unqualified density result.
Strength: The graph and sheaf specializations are technically careful about when familiar tied message-passing layers are strict subfamilies of the full orbit-parametrized operator. The sheaf construction also tracks restriction maps, invariant fiber metrics, adjoints, and stabilizer transports rather than assuming all fibers transform trivially.
Strength: The categorical appendix contains a valuable clarification that completed OENN equivariance is represented by naturality over an action groupoid, not by naturality over the thin poset category itself. The objectwise correspondence between equivariant maps and natural families is stated cleanly.
Weakness (reviewer-r3-W1): The central order-equivariant framing is broader than the actual equivariance constraint established in the main theory. Definition 2.2 is ordinary equivariance for a finite group acting on the total poset-indexed bundle, and the paper later explicitly states that the thin poset category does not provide the OENN embedding. The order supplies useful masks, incidences, covers, and sheaf structure, but the full UAT itself is not visibly order-dependent. This creates a major originality and significance uncertainty: the authors should identify the theorem content that is genuinely beyond a generic finite-group equivariant UAT, compare it theorem-by-theorem with the closest such results, or narrow the claims. I do not view this as a demonstrated algebraic error in Theorem 3.3.
Weakness (reviewer-r3-W2): The general CENN claim is not independently assessable from this submission at the advertised scope. The CENN definition delegates the arrow-bundle generators to prior work, Theorem A.3 invokes standard CENN hypotheses without a proof here, and the later finite-realization argument carefully limits itself to selected finite action-groupoid approximants. The OENN-to-action-groupoid correspondence is useful, but the stronger statements that CENN gives the general form of equivariant networks and infinitary UATs require the complete primitive definitions, assumptions, and proof, or substantially narrower wording that treats them as imported background.
Weakness (reviewer-r3-W3): The EMG-IMU comparison does not currently support the claim of substantial outperformance or improved generalization. The statistical validator found that TemporalCNN used a different split and metric, received less than two-thirds of the proposed method's compute budget and materially fewer tuning trials, and that subject-2 appeared in both training and held-out data. These are not like-for-like conditions, and the subject overlap is a direct threat to held-out validity. Resolution requires a subject-disjoint fixed split, identical preprocessing and metric definitions, matched or sensitivity-tested compute and tuning budgets, and complete run-level protocol disclosure before interpreting the performance gap.
Weakness (reviewer-r3-W4): Even after repairing split and baseline parity, the empirical evidence is too thin for a generalization claim: fewer than two distinct seeds are recorded, no uncertainty estimate is reported, eight comparisons lack a multiplicity correction, and the tested evidence does not cover distribution shift or hardware variation. Repeated independent runs with per-run values and confidence intervals, a declared primary comparison or justified correction, and narrower scope would resolve this concern.
Weakness (reviewer-r3-W5): The universality constructions do not establish practical or sample efficiency. The pair-state local construction stores a source-indexed copy of the total input at every carrier and needs diameter-many propagation layers, while the full proof uses a global broadcast. A complexity analysis and experiments comparing memory, parameter count, runtime, and accuracy against ordinary and higher-order message passing would clarify when the additional expressivity is usable rather than only existential.","soundness":3,"presentation":2,"significance":3,"originality":2,"overallRecommendation":3,"confidence":3,"questions":["Which exact theorem or architecture property depends essentially on the poset order rather than only on the finite group representation and a chosen invariant relation, and how does it differ from the closest finite-group equivariant UAT?","Can the authors provide or rerun the EMG-IMU study with a subject-disjoint fixed split, identical metrics and preprocessing, matched compute and tuning budgets for all baselines, multiple independent seeds, and run-level uncertainty?","Can the paper state all CENN primitive definitions and analytic hypotheses needed for Theorem A.3, and clearly separate results proved in this submission from results imported from prior CENN work?","What are the asymptotic and measured memory, parameter, and runtime costs of pair-state OENN relative to ordinary message passing and standard higher-order or tensorized GNNs on representative graph or sheaf domains?"],"limitations":"The proved OENN results concern finite posets, finite groups, finite-dimensional fibers, and compact invariant input domains. Local universality additionally requires a strongly connected communication relation, source-labeled state whose width scales with the total input dimension at every carrier, and depth at least the directed diameter; no sample-complexity or computational-efficiency guarantee follows. The general CENN theorem is not self-contained in this paper. Empirical support is confined to one wearable benchmark and, according to the supplied statistical validation, has split, baseline-parity, repeated-run, uncertainty, multiplicity, and robustness-scope limitations. My originality judgment also carries uncertainty because the allowed reference validation does not provide a clean verification of all recent closest-work claims.","ethicalConcerns":"The empirical appendix uses human wearable biosignal data but does not discuss consent provenance, privacy handling, demographic coverage, or dataset-license constraints. Because the paper appears to reuse cited public datasets rather than collect new data, this is a reporting and scope concern, not evidence of misconduct."}

payload.reason: Independent initial review on frozen paper 34584.

payload.content:
{"summary":"The paper studies neural networks on finite posets equipped with a finite group action and equivariant vector-space fibers. It characterizes equivariant linear maps through pair-orbit transporter and stabilizer constraints, builds nonlinear layers using Reynolds averaging and relation or pair-state aggregation, proves full and diameter-depth local universal approximation results, specializes the construction to fixed graphs and cellular or simplicial sheaves, and relates completed OENN maps to CENN natural transformations over action groupoids. An appendix also reports an EMG-IMU classification comparison for a Grothendieck CENN.","strengthsAndWeaknesses":"Strength: The linear theory is explicit and traceable: the transporter law, orbit parametrization, and stabilizer-intertwiner condition give a useful description that handles nontrivial fiber actions rather than only permutation features. Representative exact checks found the block shapes consistent and the two-site transporter specialization valid.
Strength: The paper cleanly distinguishes unrestricted orbital mixing, anonymous relation message passing, and source-labeled pair-state propagation. The full UAT and the communication-radius lower bound plus diameter-depth construction form a coherent account of why bounded-depth anonymous locality is insufficient and how source identity restores worst-case universality.
Strength: The graph and sheaf sections are technically informative. They distinguish full pair-orbit parameterization from coarser conventional tying, and they derive upward and downward sheaf blocks using restriction maps, invariant metrics, and adjoint naturality rather than asserting the specialization only informally.
Strength: The action-groupoid discussion correctly separates the role of the poset order from the role of the symmetry action and carefully types completed maps, pair-state bundles, and auxiliary branch constructions over the relevant finite G-sets.
Weakness (reviewer-r4-W1): The empirical claims are not reliable as submitted. The frozen audit found an exact subject identifier shared between training and held-out data, and it found that TemporalCNN was compared under different compute, tuning, split, and metric conditions. It also found fewer than two recorded seeds, no uncertainty estimates, and eight comparisons without multiplicity correction. The submission itself provides little protocol detail beyond the result paragraph and table. These findings do not refute the OENN theory, but they leave the claims of substantial outperformance and improved generalization unsupported. Resolution requires a subject-disjoint split manifest, like-for-like baseline reruns, run-level results across multiple seeds, uncertainty reporting, and a declared multiplicity policy.
Weakness (reviewer-r4-W2): The local universality construction is communication-local but not resource-local: every carrier stores source-labeled slots whose width equals the entire input dimension, propagation uses diameter-many inter-site layers, and exact compilation uses graph-specific length-D walk-count rescaling. The paper states the width cost but gives no parameter, memory, or runtime bounds, no orbit/stabilizer construction algorithm, and no implementation demonstrating feasibility. This limits the practical significance of the pair-state result even if the existence proof is correct. A complexity analysis and an executable example comparing pair-state OENN with lower-order alternatives would resolve the concern.
Weakness (reviewer-r4-W3): The broader CENN contribution is not self-contained enough to support the strongest 'general form' claims. Definition A.2 delegates essential arrow-bundle generators to a recent external work, Theorem A.3 invokes standard CENN hypotheses and a prior UAT, and the OENN-CENN equivalence then relies on choosing approximants from that construction. Moreover, the controlled reference audit could not confirm admissible identities for several central CENN self-citations; its findings are not proof of nonexistence, but they prevent independent verification here. The paper should state all required generators and analytic hypotheses precisely, provide a self-contained finite-groupoid theorem or proof, and supply verifiable source identities for the cited CENN results.
Weakness (reviewer-r4-W4): The novelty boundary of 'order equivariance' is insufficiently sharp. Definition 2.2 and the full UAT concern ordinary finite-group equivariance on the total feature space, and the proof obtains universality by broadcasting the complete input and applying stabilizer-equivariant MLPs; the order is not used in that argument. The paper later correctly explains that equivariance comes from the action groupoid while the poset supplies masks, covers, incidence, and sheaf structure. The introduction and contribution claims should therefore distinguish generic finite-group equivariant approximation from the genuinely order-specific locality and sheaf constructions, and compare each part against verified prior finite-group and higher-order equivariant UAT results.","soundness":2,"presentation":2,"significance":2,"originality":2,"overallRecommendation":3,"confidence":3,"questions":["Can the authors provide the exact subject-level train, validation, and test manifest, explain the reported subject-2 overlap, and rerun all results on a demonstrably subject-disjoint split?","Can the authors rerun GrothendieckCENN, TemporalCNN, and InvariantTransformer with identical splits, metric definitions, compute and tuning budgets, multiple recorded seeds, uncertainty intervals, and an explicit multiplicity policy?","Can the authors give a self-contained finite-action-groupoid statement of the CENN generators and UAT assumptions used by Lemma A.6 and Corollary A.7, together with verifiable identities for the central CENN references?","Which results are genuinely order-specific rather than consequences of generic finite-group equivariant approximation, and how do their assumptions and expressivity compare with the closest verified higher-order GNN and sheaf-network universality results?","What are the parameter count, peak memory, runtime, and construction cost for pair-state cover-local OENN as functions of the poset size, fiber dimensions, orbit count, group size, and Hasse diameter?"],"limitations":"The theory assumes finite posets, finite symmetry groups, finite-dimensional fibers, compact invariant approximation domains, and continuous non-polynomial activations. Pair-state local universality additionally requires strong connectivity or bidirectional connected covers, depth scaling with graph diameter, source-labeled memory that replicates the global input dimension at every carrier, and graph-specific compilation constants. The submission does not evaluate approximate or unknown symmetries, dynamic domains, implementation cost, or robustness. Its CENN generalization depends on externally specified generators and hypotheses, and its sole empirical example is compromised by split and protocol concerns.","ethicalConcerns":"None reported."}

payload.reason: Independent initial review on frozen paper 34584.

payload.content:
{"title":"Author rebuttal to Reviewer R1","text":"reviewer-r1-W1: We agree that the equivariance condition in Definition 2.2 is finite-group equivariance on the total direct-sum bundle, not naturality with respect to the thin poset category. The submitted paper already states that the order supplies invariant incidence relations, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restrictions, while the action groupoid supplies equivariance; the full UAT itself uses a global broadcast. The submitted evidence does not establish a literature-wide theorem-level separation from generic finite-group equivariant UATs, especially when the automorphism group is trivial. We will narrow the framing and present the order-specific content as the structured/local architecture classes, diameter-depth compilation, and sheaf constructions rather than as a different input-output symmetry condition.

reviewer-r1-W2: The exact class covered by the submitted sheaf UAT is the full OENN on total sheaf-valued fibers, which permits global orbital mixing, and, under connectedness, its cover-local source-labeled pair-state compilation with diameter-scale depth. The paper explicitly distinguishes these from ordinary restriction/adjoint-based local sheaf diffusion or message passing, which is not claimed universal. We agree the headline wording is too broad and will narrow it to this sheaf-indexed OENN completion.

reviewer-r1-W3: The submission is not self-contained for the broad CENN framework: Definition A.2 delegates the arrow-bundle generators and Theorem A.3 imports the general UAT hypotheses. What is developed in the submitted text is the finite action-groupoid account of completed OENN equivariance and a selected finite-realization statement; the text also limits the claim to approximants chosen inside the OENN primitive class rather than arbitrary CENN scalar gates. We will mark the general CENN material as imported background, separate it from the finite OENN/action-groupoid results developed here, and narrow the general-form wording.

reviewer-r1-W4: We acknowledge that the submitted EMG-IMU evidence does not support the stated claims of substantial outperformance or improved generalization. The author-visible validation catalog identifies a non-like-for-like baseline protocol, subject overlap between training and held-out data, fewer than two recorded seeds, no uncertainty estimate, no multiplicity correction, and limited validation breadth. The paper provides only the result paragraph and table for these claims. We will remove or sharply qualify the outperformance and generalization language; we do not claim a corrected result.

reviewer-r1-W5: The submitted theorem is existential and already states its main structural cost: pair-state variables indexed by carrier and source, per-carrier hidden width equal to the total input dimension, diameter-many inter-site propagation layers, and graph-specific walk-count rescaling in the exact broadcast compilation. We acknowledge that the submission provides no parameter, runtime, sample-complexity, optimization, stability, or implementation study. We will make clear that universality does not establish practical efficiency.

reviewer-r1-Q1: The submitted evidence supports a scope distinction but not a new verified theorem-by-theorem comparison with all closest finite-group or higher-order UATs. The full theorem approximates continuous G-equivariant maps for a finite group acting on a poset-indexed bundle; the order enters through invariant relations, locality, cover propagation, and sheaf structure, not through a stronger equivariance equation. A broader novelty comparison would require new literature research, so we will not manufacture it and will instead narrow the manuscript claims.

reviewer-r1-Q2: Universality is not proved for conventional local sheaf diffusion alone. It is proved for the full sheaf-indexed OENN and for the source-labeled pair-state cover-local completion under the stated connectedness, depth, and memory conditions. We will make that exact architecture class explicit wherever the sheaf UAT is summarized.

reviewer-r1-Q3: Within the manuscript, Definition A.2 and Theorem A.3 are imported from prior CENN work, while Proposition A.4, Lemma A.6, and Corollary A.7 form the submitted finite action-groupoid/OENN development. This identifies the manuscript's dependency boundary, not a verified literature-wide novelty claim. The submitted evidence does not contain complete definitions of all arrow-bundle generators or a self-contained proof of the general CENN UAT, so supplying that formalization or a new proof in rebuttal would exceed the evidence boundary. We will make the imported dependency explicit and confine the claimed contribution accordingly.

reviewer-r1-Q4: The requested subject-disjoint, protocol-matched rerun, exact split manifest, multiple-seed results, uncertainty intervals, and multiplicity analysis are not present in the submitted paper or author-visible catalog. We therefore cannot answer with a corrected performance claim or explain the reported subject overlap without new research and provenance. We will not speculate and will instead remove or sharply qualify the affected manuscript claims."}

{"title":"Author rebuttal to Reviewer R2","text":"reviewer-r2-W1: We agree. Equation (2) is finite-group equivariance on the total feature spaces, and the full UAT is not restricted by order or sheaf naturality. The sheaf consequence targets continuous G-equivariant maps on total sheaf fibers and is realized by full global orbital mixing or by the source-labeled pair-state completion, not by conventional local sheaf diffusion alone. We will narrow both the order-specific and sheaf-network wording to match those proved classes.

reviewer-r2-W2: We agree that the submitted lower bound is explicitly depth-limited: after at most L inter-site layers, an output depends only on its L-step in-neighborhood. It does not prove unrestricted-depth non-universality of the full relation-message-passing class or that persistent source labels are uniquely necessary. The pair-state construction is a sufficient universal completion. We will restrict the text to the depth-versus-distance obstruction and remove the unsupported unrestricted-depth and necessity wording.

reviewer-r2-W3: The submission is not self-contained for the broad CENN framework: Definition A.2 delegates the arrow-bundle generators and Theorem A.3 imports the general UAT hypotheses. What is developed in the submitted text is the finite action-groupoid account of completed OENN equivariance and a selected finite-realization statement; the text also limits the claim to approximants chosen inside the OENN primitive class rather than arbitrary CENN scalar gates. We will mark the general CENN material as imported background, separate it from the finite OENN/action-groupoid results developed here, and narrow the general-form wording.

reviewer-r2-W4: We acknowledge that the submitted EMG-IMU evidence does not support the stated claims of substantial outperformance or improved generalization. The author-visible validation catalog identifies a non-like-for-like baseline protocol, subject overlap between training and held-out data, fewer than two recorded seeds, no uncertainty estimate, no multiplicity correction, and limited validation breadth. The paper provides only the result paragraph and table for these claims. We will remove or sharply qualify the outperformance and generalization language; we do not claim a corrected result.

reviewer-r2-Q1: The exact target of the full theorem is all continuous G-equivariant maps between the total bundles; it is not further constrained by order arrows or sheaf restriction naturality. Order and sheaf structure enter through the selected invariant masks, cover-local communication, and sheaf-type layer constructions, while universality uses either full mixing or source-labeled pair states. We will make that distinction explicit.

reviewer-r2-Q2: No unrestricted-depth indistinguishability counterexample is contained in the submitted evidence. Producing one would be a new proof, so we cannot provide it in rebuttal. We will narrow the claim to Theorem 3.5's established finite-depth dependency lower bound.

reviewer-r2-Q3: Within the manuscript, Definition A.2 and Theorem A.3 are imported from prior CENN work, while Proposition A.4, Lemma A.6, and Corollary A.7 form the submitted finite action-groupoid/OENN development. This identifies the manuscript's dependency boundary, not a verified literature-wide novelty claim. The submitted evidence does not contain complete definitions of all arrow-bundle generators or a self-contained proof of the general CENN UAT, so supplying that formalization or a new proof in rebuttal would exceed the evidence boundary. We will make the imported dependency explicit and confine the claimed contribution accordingly.

reviewer-r2-Q4: The requested subject-disjoint, protocol-matched rerun, exact split manifest, multiple-seed results, uncertainty intervals, and multiplicity analysis are not present in the submitted paper or author-visible catalog. We therefore cannot answer with a corrected performance claim or explain the reported subject overlap without new research and provenance. We will not speculate and will instead remove or sharply qualify the affected manuscript claims."}

{"title":"Author rebuttal to Reviewer R3","text":"reviewer-r3-W1: We agree that the equivariance condition in Definition 2.2 is finite-group equivariance on the total direct-sum bundle, not naturality with respect to the thin poset category. The submitted paper already states that the order supplies invariant incidence relations, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restrictions, while the action groupoid supplies equivariance; the full UAT itself uses a global broadcast. The submitted evidence does not establish a literature-wide theorem-level separation from generic finite-group equivariant UATs, especially when the automorphism group is trivial. Confidence remains 4 because the formal scope and dependency boundaries are now clearer, but the response adds no new proof or empirical verification and does not remove the reviewer's stated systems-scalability blind spot.
